items.title: Vance says US to pursue Iran nuclear deal regardless of Israel position
German Industrial Output Rises for First Time Since War Began
items.summary: German industrial production grew for the first time since war broke out in Iran, fueling hope that Europe’s largest economy is weathering the jump in energy costs.

items.title:
There are different people around Putin. Half of them want to continue this war and the other half want to stop it. Businessmen understand that the Russian economy is in a terrible situation. 

If Western countries – Europe, Canada, and the U.S. – don’t lift their sanctions, put more pressure on the Russian shadow fleet and other sectors, and Ukraine stays strong on the battlefield, then we will be closer to peace. 

I told this businessman, who came to deliver the message about a potential framework of diplomatic negotiations, that we were ready to speak from the very beginning. We didn't want this war, and we want to stop it. 

Yesterday I told the journalists that in Anchorage, Russia and the U.S. decided to speak about Ukraine without Ukraine. It was a mistake, especially for America, because Putin weakened the U.S. position. I think he was lying to the President of the United States. 

Anyway, we are ready to speak, but we won’t give up our territory. 

From an interview with The Guardian. (2/3)

items.title: China’s Aluminum Exports Surge to Fill Global Shortfall From War
items.summary: Chinese aluminum exports surged in May, helping fill a global shortage caused by war in the Middle East. Crude oil imports continued to slump, dropping to an eight-year low, as the conflict chokes shipments from major producers.

items.title: China’s Oil Imports Plunge to Eight-Year Low on War Disruptions
items.summary: China’s buying of oil from overseas slumped to the lowest in more than eight years last month as the Iran war crimped supply and Beijing held off scrambling for replacement barrels.

items.summary: Indonesia’s central bank unexpectedly raised its benchmark interest rate in an off-cycle decision, seeking to shore up the rupiah after a selloff rattled the nation’s currency and bonds.

Bibi , you better be careful : Trump cautions Netanyahu against striking Iran to avert regional war

E3 countries will help us with anti-ballistic capabilities. By the way, I hope that we will manage to develop a European anti-ballistic system together with the UK. We are working on it. We need it, and the UK needs it. 

Secondly, sanctions. Yesterday at the E3 meeting everybody agreed that we need to strengthen sanctions on Russia. 

Thirdly, we need to move the Ukrainian army to a contract-based system. It's dangerous to be a soldier and we need to give them respectable salaries because they are giving their lives. We need additional funding for this. Currently, it's nowhere near what Russia pays its mercenaries. I do hope that our friends will help. 

From an interview with The Guardian. (3/3)

items.title: Who’s calling the shots? Netanyahu and Trump at odds over their war
items.summary:
Israel’s latest strikes on Lebanon and Iran have made clear that US President Donald Trump and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, who started the war in lockstep, want different things.
Trump had publicly warned Israel not to strike Beirut in its war with Iran-backed Hezbollah militants. When it did, on Sunday, Iran responded by firing ballistic missiles at Israel for the first time since

items.title: Trump responds to US Apache helicopter crash near Hormuz, claims Iran deal close
items.summary:
A US Army Apache attack helicopter crashed near the Strait of Hormuz, with President Donald Trump saying the two crew members abroad were “fine” after the incident involving the strategic waterway, which remains under a chokehold by Iran.

items.title: Xi Jinping’s visit to North Korean war monument evokes ‘eternal historical memory’
items.summary:
Visiting a historic mountainside in Pyongyang, Chinese President Xi Jinping emphasised the shared sacrifices and deep historical ties between China and North Korea.
On Tuesday, Xi and first lady Peng Liyuan paid tribute at the Sino-Korean Friendship Tower in Moran Hill, accompanied by North Korean leader Kim Jong-un and his wife Ri Sol-ju.
